description: Fix incorrect Claude Code MCP config path in CLI output. The CLI tells users to put their MCP config in ~/.claude/settings/mcp.json, which does not exist. Claude Code stores MCP server configs in ~/.claude.json (user scope) via `claude mcp add`. Update the CLI to show the correct path and include a ready-to-copy `claude mcp add` command.

userStories.title: Fix Claude Code MCP config path and add CLI command in start.ts
userStories.description: As a new user, I want the `opentabs start` output to show the correct Claude Code MCP config location and a copy-paste `claude mcp add` command so that I can actually connect Claude Code to OpenTabs.
acceptanceCriteria: The Claude Code entry in getMcpClientConfigs uses file path '~/.claude.json' instead of '~/.claude/settings/mcp.json'

printMcpClientConfigs prints a `claude mcp add` command for Claude Code (e.g., `claude mcp add --transport http opentabs http://127.0.0.1:9515/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer <secret>"`) in addition to the JSON snippet
The `claude mcp add` command is printed first (as the recommended approach), followed by the JSON snippet as a manual alternative
The JSON snippet still shows the correct ~/.claude.json path
userStories.notes:
The config is defined in platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts:161-198 in getMcpClientConfigs(). The file path for Claude Code is at line 169: `file: '~/.claude/settings/mcp.json'`. Change it to `file: '~/.claude.json'`.

Additionally, update printMcpClientConfigs() (line 200-238) to also print a CLI command for Claude Code. The canonical way to add an MCP server in Claude Code is:
```
claude mcp add --transport http opentabs http://127.0.0.1:<port>/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer <secret>"
```
This should be printed above the JSON block for the Claude Code entry. For other clients that don't have a CLI, just show the JSON as before.

The same getMcpClientConfigs function is imported and used in platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts at lines 5, 86, and 208 — those call sites don't need changes since they consume the return value.

userStories.title: Fix Claude Code MCP config path in doctor.ts
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs doctor`, I want it to check the correct Claude Code config location (~/.claude.json) so that the diagnostic accurately reports whether Claude Code is configured.
acceptanceCriteria: defaultMcpClientLocations() checks ~/.claude.json instead of ~/.claude/settings/mcp.json for the Claude Code entry
The check reads ~/.claude.json and looks for an 'opentabs' entry in the mcpServers field
userStories.notes:
The check is in platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:254:
```
{ name: 'Claude Code', path: join(home, '.claude', 'settings', 'mcp.json') },
```
Change to:
```
{ name: 'Claude Code', path: join(home, '.claude.json') },
```
The checkMcpClientConfig function (line 263) reads the file and checks for an 'opentabs' key in mcpServers. The ~/.claude.json file has a top-level `mcpServers` field, so the existing check logic should work if the path is corrected. Verify by reading checkMcpClientConfig to confirm it handles the JSON structure correctly.
